steps.title: Link pressure to avoidance
steps.content: The hypothesis says fish avoid obstructions more successfully when pressure fluctuations at the snout are greater. For large vortices, intermediate-headed fish have higher pressure fluctuations, while wide-headed fish have low pressure fluctuations.

steps.title: Choose the predicted contrast
steps.content: A gives only a wide-headed species, so there is no comparison. B says intermediate- and wide-headed fish bumped into obstructions equally often, which would not support the hypothesis. D compares individuals of the same intermediate-headed species. The supporting result is wide-headed fish bumping more often than intermediate-headed fish, because that pattern links lower pressure fluctuations with poorer avoidance. The answer is C.
